intent: NVIDIA's own DGX SuperPOD B300 Reference Architecture (dated 2025-10-30) shows the flagship GPU deployment shipping in standard EIA racks with three 2U rack PDUs each — a purchasable/published artifact that directly settles whether GPU racks skip PDUs today. Combined with the Grand View PDU market report attributing growth to AI/GPU workloads, the load-bearing premise ('GPU growth arrives PDU-less') is falsified now, not over time.

results.text:
DGX SuperPOD Architecture — NVIDIA SuperPOD DGX B300 Systems, Spectrum-4 Ethernet and DC Busbar Power Reference Architecture

NVIDIA SuperPOD DGX B300 Systems, Spectrum-4 Ethernet and DC Busbar Power Reference Architecture

Is this page helpful?

# DGX SuperPOD Architecture#

The DGX SuperPOD architecture is a combination of DGX systems, Ethernet networking, InfiniBand Networking, management nodes, and storage. Figure 3 the rack layout of a single SU. With DGX SuperPOD B300, we introduce the MGX-based, DC busbar powered design for the best datacenter density and efficiency.

In our reference design, for DGX B300s are within a single rack. The rack-level power consumption per rack exceeds 50 kW. The rack layout can be adjusted to meet local data center requirements, such as maximum power per rack and rack layout between DGX systems and supporting equipment to meet local needs for power and cooling distribution.

For legacy datacenter without the possibility for DC busbar, it is still possible to build SuperPOD with traditional PDU and AC powered EIA racks.

Figure 3 shows 64 x NVIDIA DGX B300 Busbar systems in MGX racks each with DC Power shelfs and MGX rack stiffeners.

Figure 3 DGX B300 in Racks#

Figure 4 shows an example management rack configuration with networking switches, management servers, storage arrays, and UFM appliances. Sizes and quantities will vary depending upon models used.

Figure 4 Management rack configuration#

This reference architecture is focused on 4

results.text:
Title: DGX SuperPOD Architecture — NVIDIA SuperPOD with DGX B300 Systems, NVIDIA Quantum-X800 InfiniBand switching and AC Power Reference Architecture

URL Source: https://docs.nvidia.com/dgx-superpod/reference-architecture/scalable-infrastructure-b300-xdr/latest/dgx-superpod-architecture.html

Published Time: Thu, 30 Oct 2025 20:30:45 GMT

## Markdown Content:
DGX SuperPOD Architecture#

The DGX SuperPOD architecture is a combination of DGX systems, Ethernet networking, InfiniBand Networking, management nodes, and storage. Figure 2 the rack layout of a single SU. With DGX SuperPOD with DGX B300 systems, we utilize standard racks and with traditional power supplies and PDUs.

In our reference design, four DGX B300 fit within a single rack. The rack-level power consumption per rack is ~56kW. The rack layout can be adjusted to meet local data center requirements, such as maximum power per rack and rack layout between DGX systems and supporting equipment to meet local needs for power and cooling distribution.

This Reference Architecture is focused on traditional PDU and AC powered EIA racks. DGX SuperPOD with DGX B300 systems is also available for more dense DB Busbar solutions as well.

Figure 2 shows 72 x NVIDIA DGX B300 PS systems in standard racks each with three (3) 2U rack PDUs for maximum redundancy. Note that depending on your data center’s capability, you might need to reduce the number of DGXs hosted on the same rack.

Figure 2 DGX B300 in Racks#

Figure 3 shows an ex

results.title: NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD: Next Generation Scalable Infrastructure for AI Factories
results.text:
## NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD: Next Generation Scalable Infrastructure for AI Factories

Reference Architecture Featuring NVIDIA DGX GB300 Systems

NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD: Next Generation Scalable Infrastructure for AI Factories | i

## Abstract

The NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD architecture has been designed to power the next generation AI factories with unparalleled performance, scalability, and innovation that supports all customers in the enterprise, higher education, research, and the public sectors. It is a physical twin of NVIDIA’s own system used by research and development teams, meaning the DGX SuperPOD customer’s infrastructure software, applications, and support have already been tested and vetted on the same architecture through NVIDIA’s internal usage.

This DGX SuperPOD Reference Architecture (RA) is based on DGX GB300 systems powered by Grace CPUs and Blackwell Ultra GPUs. The RA discusses the components that define the scalable and modular architecture of DGX SuperPOD. DGX SuperPOD is built on the concept of scalable units (SU); each SU contains 8 DGX GB300 systems, which enables rapid deployment of DGX SuperPOD of any size. The RA also includes details regarding the SU design and specifics of InfiniBand, NVLink network, Ethernet fabric topologies, storage system specifications, recommended rack layouts, and wiring guidelines.

NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD: Next Generation Scalable Infrastructure for AI Factories | ii

This RA combines the latest NVIDIA technologies to help companies an

- Report ID: GVR-1-68038-830-5
- Number of Report Pages: 120
- Format: PDF

- Historical Range: 2021 - 2025
- Forecast Period: 2026 - 2033
- Industry: Technology

## Data Center Rack Power Distribution Unit Market Summary

The global data center rack power distribution unit market size was estimated at USD 2.81 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach USD 5.87 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 9.7% from 2026 to 2033. The growth of high-performance computing (HPC), artificial intelligence (AI), and GPU-intensive workloads is driving demand for data center rack power distribution unit market.

### Key Market Trends & Insights

- North America held 38.0% revenue share of the global data center rack power distribution unit market.
- In the U.S., the expansion of colocation data centers is accelerating the demand for data center rack power distribution unit market.
- By rack type, non-intelligent segment held the largest revenue share in 2025.

### Market Size & Forecast

- 2025 Market Size: USD 2.81 Billion
- 2033 Projected Market Size: USD 5.87 Billion
- CAGR (2026-2033): 9.7%
- North America: Largest market in 2025
- Asia Pacific: Fastest grow

### DATA CENTER PDU MARKET SIZE, SHARE & TRENDS

According to Marketsandmarkets, the data center PDU market size was valued USD 3.94 biliion in 2025 and is project to reach USD 14.23 billion by 2032, growing at a CAGR of 19.5% from 2026 to 2032. The data center PDU market is driven by the expansion of hyperscale and AI-driven data centers, the increasing rack power density, and the rising adoption of intelligent PDUs for real-time monitoring and operational efficiency. Additionally, the strong focus on energy efficiency, sustainability, and reliable power distribution infrastructure is further supporting the demand for advanced PDU solutions across modern data center environments.

### MARKET SNAPSHOT TABLE

- Schneider Electric (France)
- Eaton (Ireland)
- Vertiv Group Corp. (US)
- Legrand (France)
- ABB (Switzerland)
- nVent (UK)

- The rapid growth of hyperscale data centers operated by cloud service providers is driving demand for advanced PDUs capable of managing high power loads,
